Check HarmonyOS scaffold markers in the current directory: build-profile.json5, oh-package.json5, entry/, entry/src/main/module.json5. 2. If markers are missing, create the correct scaffold by invoking the bundled create-harmony-project or create-atomic-service skill conservatively from the original prompt. 3. Read the standard key scaffold files that exist and produce a concise Scaffold Preflight Summary. Stop after the Scaffold Preflight Summary. Do not read .arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md or .arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json yet. Do not write .arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json, .arkpilot/plans/autopilot-impl.md, or feature implementation source files in this preflight pass. content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-scaffold-preflight

<Purpose>
Use this skill at the start of the UITREE DAG Plan Agent session, before `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md` and `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json` exist.

It creates or verifies the HarmonyOS scaffold, reads the standard scaffold files, emits a concise `Scaffold Preflight Summary`, then stops. It does not write a handoff file and does not implement product features.

This skill owns project creation and scaffold verification for the UITREE execution flow. Later build skills should consume the verified scaffold instead of invoking project creation or running a duplicate scaffold preflight phase.
</Purpose>

<Inputs>
- User's original product prompt.
- Current project directory.
</Inputs>

<Project_Type_Selection>
Choose conservatively from the original prompt:

- Use `/ArkPilot:create-atomic-service` only when the prompt explicitly asks for `元服务`, `原子化服务`, `atomic service`, `Atomic Service`, `服务卡片`, or a clearly equivalent atomic-service target.
- Otherwise default to `/ArkPilot:create-harmony-project`.
- If a HarmonyOS scaffold already exists, do not recreate it. Infer the current scaffold type from actual project files when practical, and continue with pre-reading.
</Project_Type_Selection>

<Workflow>
1. Check scaffold markers in the current directory:
   - `build-profile.json5`
   - `oh-package.json5`
   - `entry/`
   - `entry/src/main/module.json5`
2. If any marker is missing, invoke the selected creation skill in the current directory:
   - Regular app: `/ArkPilot:create-harmony-project`
   - Atomic service / 元服务: `/ArkPilot:create-atomic-service`
3. After creation, verify the same markers again.
4. Pre-read the standard scaffold files that exist. Missing optional files should be reported in the summary, not treated as fatal:
   - `build-profile.json5`
   - `oh-package.json5`
   - `AppScope/app.json5`
   - `entry/src/main/module.json5`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/profile/main_pages.json`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/entryability/EntryAbility.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/color.json`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/string.json`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/float.json`
5. If the scaffold contains the fixed bottom-tab shell files below, read them as scaffold evidence and mention the detected shell in the summary. Do not validate product behavior or rewrite the shell:
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets`
6. Output only a concise `Scaffold Preflight Summary` and stop.
</Workflow>

<Boundaries>
- Do not read `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md`.
- Do not read `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json`.
- Do not write `.arkpilot/plans/autopilot-impl.md`.
- Do not write any handoff or cache artifact for the preflight summary.
- Do not edit feature source files beyond scaffold creation by the existing project creation skill.
- Do not launch the app, start an emulator, run runtime UI verification, or take screenshots.
</Boundaries>

<Summary_Format>
End with this shape:

```text
Scaffold Preflight Summary
- Project type: app | atomic-service | existing-unknown
- Scaffold status: existing | created | blocked
- Marker evidence: ...
- Entry ability: ...
- Main page registry: ...
- Initial page: ...
- Fixed bottom-tab shell: detected | absent | partial
- Resource files present: ...
- Missing optional files: ...
- Notes for later impl plan: ...
```
</Summary_Format>

<Exit_Conditions>
- A valid scaffold exists, or a clear blocker is reported.
- Standard scaffold files have been read when present.
- The final response contains `Scaffold Preflight Summary`.
- No spec, UI tree, implementation plan, feature source edits, or handoff file were produced by this skill.
</Exit_Conditions>

addedLines: - ArkPilot:analyst: Requirements analyst for autopilot-standalone (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:architect: Technical architect and plan reviewer for autopilot-standalone (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:code-reviewer: Code reviewer for autopilot-standalone validation phase (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:critic: Plan critic for autopilot-standalone — final quality gate before execution (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:executor: Agent from ArkPilot plugin (Tools: All tools)
- ArkPilot:explore: Codebase explorer for ArkPilot deep-interview brownfield mode (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash)

- ArkPilot:harmonyos-ui-validator: Validate HarmonyOS app UI against a test spec or checklist. Use proactively when the user asks to verify screens, flows, layout, controls, visibility, or interaction results in a HarmonyOS app. (Tools: All tools)
- ArkPilot:security-reviewer: Security reviewer for autopilot-standalone validation phase (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

Use this skill when the user wants to map a spec to HarmonyOS system APIs, Kits, permissions, media/camera/audio/file/location/network/AI capabilities, or explore the local HarmonyOS docs directory structure. - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/create-harmony-project

# Create Harmony Project

Use this skill when the user asks for things like:

- "创建一个鸿蒙工程"
- "初始化一个 HarmonyOS 空项目"
- "给我一个 ArkTS 空白应用骨架"

This skill is for scaffolding a HarmonyOS project into the current workspace from a bundled template. It is not a general HarmonyOS coding guide.

## Workflow

1. **Do not check the workspace contents beforehand.**
2. **Do not ask the user for confirmation.**
3. Use the current workspace as the project directory instead of creating a new subdirectory.
4. Default the displayed app name to the current workspace directory name.
5. Only pass `--bundle-name`, `--vendor`, or other metadata overrides when the user explicitly asks for them. Do not ask proactively.
6. Do not handwrite the scaffold from scratch when the bundled template is sufficient.
7. Run the scaffold script immediately and return its output:

```bash
python3 SKILL_DIRECTORY/scripts/create_project.py "$PWD"
```

Optional overrides:

```bash
python3 SKILL_DIRECTORY/scripts/create_project.py "$PWD" \
  --project-name WorkspaceName \
  --bundle-name com.example.newapp \
  --vendor example
```

## What the script does

- copies the contents of `assets/empty-project/` into the requested workspace directory
- skips local machine files such as `.DS_Store`, `.idea`, and `local.properties`
- allows the destination directory to already exist, but stops on file collisions instead of overwriting existing project files
- updates:
  - `AppScope/app.json5`
  - `AppScope/resources/base/element/string.json`
  - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/string.json`

## Guardrails

- Treat `assets/empty-project/` as the source of truth for the scaffold.
- Prefer using the current workspace name as the app name when the user does not provide one.
- Unless the user explicitly specifies metadata such as `bundleName` or `vendor`, keep the template defaults.
- If the current workspace already contains colliding project files, stop with a clear error instead of overwriting them.

39	@Component
40	export struct BottomTabBar {
41	  @Prop activeTab: TabId;
42	  onTap: (tab: TabId) => void = () => {};
43	
44	  build() {
45	    Row() {
46	      ForEach(TABS, (tab: TabDescriptor) => {
47	        Column() {
48	          SvgIcon({
49	            variant: tab.icon,
50	            iconSize: 24,
51	            color: this.activeTab === tab.id ? Theme.brandPrimary : Theme.textQuaternary,
52	            strokeWidth: 2
53	          })
54	          Text(tab.label)
55	            .fontSize(11)
56	            .fontWeight(FontWeight.Medium)
57	            .fontColor(this.activeTab === tab.id ? Theme.brandPrimary : Theme.textQuaternary)
58	            .margin({ top: 4 })
59	        }
60	        .id(tab.id)
61	        .padding({ left: 12, right: 12, top: 6, bottom: 6 })
62	        .onClick(() => this.onTap(tab.id))
63	        .layoutWeight(1)
64	        .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)
65	      }, (tab: TabDescriptor) => tab.id)
66	    }
67	    .width('100%')
68	    .height(Theme.bottomTabHeight)
69	    .padding({ bottom: 8 })
70	    .backgroundColor('rgba(255,255,255,0.94)')
71	    .border({ width: { top: 1 }, color: { top: Theme.borderSoft } })
72	    .justifyContent(FlexAlign.SpaceAround)
73	    .alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)
74	  }
75	}

Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id

<Purpose>
Implement the app from UITREE planning artifacts, including behavior-contract planning, design-alignment integration, and build stabilization.

Primary inputs:
- `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md`
- `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json`
- optional `.arkpilot/plans/design-alignment.md`
- optional hook-injected "UITREE DAG VISUAL ALIGNMENT PASS" context when the design lane finishes while this session is active
- optional `.arkpilot/designs/page-*.html`

This skill owns code implementation and build stabilization only. It does not run runtime QA, launch devices, inspect runtime UI trees, or take screenshots.
</Purpose>

<First_Principles>
1. Assume the HarmonyOS scaffold already exists before behavior planning or implementation.
2. Treat `ui-tree.json` as a lightweight semantic ID/test-target protocol, not as a full layout tree.
3. Treat `ui-behavior.json` as the behavior source of truth. If it is missing or stale, create or refresh it before feature code.
4. Use this skill for UITREE execution orchestration: artifact reading, execution flow control, semantic ID binding, implementation order, static checks, and build stabilization.
5. Use `arkui-ui-patterns` for reusable ArkUI structure choices such as Navigation, Tabs, state ownership, persistence, dialogs, lists, forms, and component split.
6. When visual artifacts are absent, implement headless feature logic first. Once design alignment arrives, append visual integration and final build to the task list and continue in the same run when possible.
7. Visible controls in the final UI must connect to real state, validation, persistence, navigation, feedback, or service effects.
</First_Principles>

<Scaffold_Shell_Contract>
If the scaffold contains the fixed bottom-tab shell files below, treat them as the existing shell contract before implementation planning:

```text
entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets
entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets
entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets
entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets
entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets
```

For this shell contract:
- Keep `Theme.ets` as the shared semantic theme-token file unless the user explicitly asks for a different theme architecture.
- Keep `SvgIcon.ets` as the shared icon entry/wrapper; update variant mapping and assets when needed, but do not scatter rawfile icon paths across page files by default.
- Keep `TabTypes.ets` as the shared `TabId` source of truth.
- Keep `BottomTabBar.ets` as the fixed bottom-tab component with the stable public API `activeTab` and `onTap`.
- Keep `Index.ets` as the app shell that owns tab state and surface switching.
- Preserve the existing shell pattern: tab surfaces stay coordinated through `Index.ets` plus `BottomTabBar.ets`; change content, page imports, tab labels/ids/icons, theme tokens, and icon variant mappings as needed, but do not replace this shell with native `Tabs + TabContent` unless the user explicitly asks for a navigation rewrite.
</Scaffold_Shell_Contract>

<Behavior_Contract_Planning>
Write `.arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json` before implementation.

Required top-level fields:
- `uiTreePath`: `".arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json"`
- `dataModel`: entities, settings/form state, computed state, persistence-relevant fields
- `services`: storage, API, notification, permission, or helper boundaries needed by the spec
- `stateMachine`: behavior states and transitions, not visual decoration
- `interactionFlows`: triggers, inputs, ordered steps, side effects, expected state, and error paths
- `testTargets`: stable semantic IDs for clicks, inputs, and assertions
- optional `changeRequests`: missing target IDs or behavior/design mismatches

Use `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md` for product scope and `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json` for semantic IDs/events/bindings.
</Behavior_Contract_Planning>

<Execution_Flow>
Start with behavior-contract planning and logic implementation. Do not require a hard upfront mode decision.

When visual artifacts are not yet available, keep progressing on headless feature logic and avoid committing to final layout structure.

When `.arkpilot/plans/design-alignment.md`, `.arkpilot/designs/page-*.html`, or hook-injected `UITREE DAG VISUAL ALIGNMENT PASS` context becomes available, treat that as the signal to schedule visual integration and the final build.

If visual artifacts arrive while logic work is in progress, finish the current coherent logic change, append a visual integration/final build task to the end of the task list, and continue in the same run when possible. Do not preserve temporary layout structure just to maintain an earlier intermediate UI shape.
</Execution_Flow>

<Semantic_ID_Rules>
Read `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json` when it exists.

For `arkpilot-uitree/v2` semantic protocol:
- preserve surface IDs, page IDs, route IDs, tab IDs
- preserve `semanticTargets[].id`
- preserve event names
- preserve test target IDs
</Semantic_ID_Rules>

<Headless_Logic_Work>
Goal: implement compile-checkable feature logic without committing to visual layout.

Implement:
- model/type files
- service/helper/storage files
- notification/permission/API adapters, using explicit stubs only when the behavior contract/spec allows them
- controller/view-model/state-machine files that expose state and event methods from `ui-behavior.json`
- validation, persistence, derived data, error paths, and side effects

Avoid:
- final page/component files
- layout-bearing thin shells
- visual tokens, SVG extraction, icon systems, cards, chips, tab visuals, Canvas, gradients, shadows, glass, typography polish

Static verification:
- Run `CheckArktsFiles` on changed `.ets` logic files.
- Do not keep fixing toward a full build during headless logic work unless the user explicitly asks for an early build.
- If design alignment arrives before the handoff, do not output the headless logic stop/idle handoff; append the visual integration/build task and continue.
</Headless_Logic_Work>

<Visual_Integration_Work>
Goal: implement final ArkTS UI and run the final build.

Read:
- `ui-behavior.json`
- semantic UI protocol
- `design-alignment.md`
- relevant `page-*.html`
- the selected `arkui-ui-patterns` references for app shell, navigation, tabs, state, lists, forms, dialogs, and data
- `/Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id/references/HTML2ArkUIGuide.md` when mapping HTML to ArkUI
- `/Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id/references/svg-icon-rules.md` before extracting/using SVG icons
- `entry/src/main/resources/rawfile/svg-style-manifest.json` and `entry/src/main/ets/common/svg/SvgStyleMap.ets` when they exist before styling HTML-derived SVG icons in ArkTS

Do not read `.arkpilot/designs/DESIGN*.md` by default during visual integration. Treat `page-*.html` plus `design-alignment.md` as the visual execution contract; fall back to the final `DESIGN*.md` only when those artifacts omit a visual detail needed for implementation.

Implement:
- final Pages/Components from HTML artifacts
- semantic `.id(...)` bindings for action/input/assertion/test targets
- final visual hierarchy, spacing, typography, icons, colors, imagery, and state styling
- UI event wiring to the existing controller/state machine/services
- any missing logic discovered by visual flows
- complete usable screen content for every implemented final screen; do not ship `coming soon`, empty placeholder, or decorative-only pages during visual integration

Rules:
- Reuse existing models/services/controllers from headless logic work.
- Replace temporary compile-anchor UI freely.
- Do not bake HTML sample data into app defaults unless required by the spec.
- Do not invent decorative icons when HTML does not define them.
- If the scaffold already includes `entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets`, preserve that fixed bottom-tab shell structure during visual integration.
- In that scaffold shape, keep `Theme.ets` as the shared token source, keep `SvgIcon.ets` as the shared icon wrapper, keep `TabTypes.ets` as the shared tab contract, keep `BottomTabBar.ets` as the reusable bottom bar, and keep `Index.ets` as the shell that owns tab switching and non-tab surface replacement.
- Update the shell conservatively: change page imports, tab metadata, tab/content wiring, theme tokens, icon variant mapping, and related asset usage as needed for the real app, but do not rewrite the shell into native `Tabs + TabContent`, inline rawfile icon paths throughout page files, or move BottomTabBar behavior into unrelated page files unless the user explicitly asks.
- When HTML-derived SVG assets are present, prefer the prepared project artifacts under `entry/src/main/resources/rawfile/` and `entry/src/main/ets/common/svg/` instead of re-guessing icon colors in ArkTS. Treat `entry/src/main/resources/rawfile/svg-style-manifest.json` and `entry/src/main/ets/common/svg/SvgStyleMap.ets` as the source of truth for SVG default color, fill/stroke mode, size, and usage context.
- If project-level SVG style artifacts exist, wire icon usage through `SvgStyleMap.ets` or otherwise honor the same values. Do not replace HTML-derived SVG colors with unrelated fallback tokens unless the design explicitly changes them.
- SVG gate: for any manifest entry with `paint_mode: "stroke"` or empty `fill_color`, never chain `.fillColor()` on `Image($rawfile(file_name))`. For selected/unselected/dynamic states, switch between the prepared rawfile variants instead of tinting the same SVG. Before final build, grep edited `.ets` files for `Image($rawfile(` near `.fillColor(` and remove violations for stroke/rawfile icons.
- Do not change behavior contracts silently; record mismatches in the handoff.

Final verification:
- Optionally run `CheckArktsFiles` on edited `.ets` files before the final build.
- Run `deveco-cli build --project "$PWD"` once after final visual integration is complete. Use the current workspace root as the project path.
- Fix build failures until the primary build passes.
</Visual_Integration_Work>

<Data_Implementation_Requirements>
- Follow the data strategy from `.arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json` and `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md`. Do not invent a new backend, API, schema, or persistence model unless the behavior contract/spec leaves it genuinely unspecified.
- For self-contained apps, implement real local behavior instead of static decorative data. Use in-memory state for transient UI state and a dedicated local service layer for durable user data when persistence is required.
- For local persistence, prefer the API/reference path already prepared for the project, such as `.arkpilot/api-reference/`, when it exists. If no API reference exists, implement through a dedicated service file and consult local HarmonyOS references only when needed.
- For external-data apps, use the endpoint, mock strategy, or data source named by the spec/behavior contract. If the required data source needs an API key, account, network setup, or unavailable service, use realistic mock data behind the same service interface and document the fallback in the handoff.
- Do not bake HTML sample data into app defaults unless the spec or behavior contract explicitly requires seeded data.
- Keep model/types explicit. Use named classes or interfaces for shared state, service results, list rows, and persisted records.
- Implement required empty, loading, error, and populated states when the plan/spec calls for them or when the chosen data source can naturally produce those states.
- Do not hardcode secrets, tokens, API keys, private URLs, or user-specific credentials.
</Data_Implementation_Requirements>

<Interaction_Requirements>
- Every clickable final UI element should do something meaningful: navigate, filter, refresh, toggle, save, reset, show details, open a sheet/dialog, or provide clear feedback.
- Visible controls must map to real state changes, persistence, validation, feedback, navigation, or service effects.
- Do not leave decorative placeholders for planned core interactions.
- If a semantic event cannot be implemented because the contract is incomplete, keep the UI non-misleading and record the missing behavior in the handoff.
- Do not silently rename semantic event names from `ui-tree.json` or flow targets from `ui-behavior.json`.
</Interaction_Requirements>

<ArkTS_Implementation_Rules>
- Choose Navigation, Tabs, state ownership, persistence, dialogs, forms, lists, and component boundaries through `arkui-ui-patterns` before emitting UI code.
- Generate `.ets` files according to currently available inputs. During headless logic work, prioritize shared types/data/service/controller modules. Once visual artifacts are available, generate final screen/component files from HTML artifacts and design alignment.
- If the existing scaffold already provides `Theme.ets`, `SvgIcon.ets`, `TabTypes.ets`, `BottomTabBar.ets`, and `Index.ets` as a fixed bottom-tab shell, preserve those file roles and extend them instead of replacing them with a different shell architecture.
- For that scaffold shape, keep `Theme.ets` as the semantic token layer, keep `SvgIcon.ets` as the shared icon indirection layer, and keep `BottomTabBar.ets` layout hierarchy and public API stable; only change tab ids, labels, icons, theme usage, icon mappings, and related content wiring as needed.
- Do not move the shared `TabId` union back into `Index.ets`, a controller file, or page-local files when `TabTypes.ets` already exists.
- Do not bypass `SvgIcon.ets` by hardcoding rawfile icon paths across multiple page files when the scaffold already provides the shared icon wrapper, unless the user explicitly asks for a different icon architecture.
- Use realistic interactions instead of decorative placeholders.
- Do not use emoji or pictographic Unicode as icons in ArkTS `Text`, tabs, buttons, pills, stats, summaries, or final UI.
- During visual integration, apply prepared SVG assets and style metadata instead of hand-guessing icon colors, paint mode, or default size.
- Apply semantic `.id(...)` values to page roots, tabs, action targets, input targets, assertion targets, list/state targets, and repeated item patterns where the protocol or behavior contract requires stable selectors.
- Do not invent a different ID for an element that already has a semantic target. Do not rename kebab-case IDs to ArkTS-style camelCase.
- When lint errors or build diagnostics appear, you may consult `arkts-lint-build-repair` for repair guidance.
</ArkTS_Implementation_Rules>

<Team_Based_Execution>
Team tools may be used when useful.

- The current session is the leader.
- The leader decides whether to create teammates, how to split work, and when to stop using team parallelism.
- Split work by file, directory, component, service, or feature boundary.
- The leader should define shared interfaces, models, props, callbacks, and ownership boundaries before assigning parallel work.
- Teammates may write code only in assigned scopes. If they need to touch another scope, they must report that first.
- Only the leader runs `CheckArktsFiles`, `deveco-cli build --project "$PWD"`, package, deploy, install, launch, or runtime tools.
- Avoid parallel edits to the same file.
- Teammates should report completion, changed files, assumptions, blockers, and risks through `sendmessage`.
- The leader owns final integration, conflict resolution, verification, and handoff.
- If work is tightly coupled or uncertain, prefer one leader-owned implementation path instead of spawning teammates.
</Team_Based_Execution>

<Implementation_Order>
1. Read `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md` and `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json`.
2. If `entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets`, `entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets`, and `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets` exist, read them early and treat them as the scaffold shell contract.

5. Identify model/type, service, controller/state-machine, route, and semantic ID/event work from the behavior contract.
6. Implement logic modules first.
7. If design alignment arrives at any point, add visual integration and final build as the final task in the task list.
8. When design alignment has not arrived, run `CheckArktsFiles` and stop after a clean static check or clearly reported diagnostics; do not claim final UI/build completion.
9. Once visual artifacts are available, read them and inspect any prepared SVG artifacts under `entry/src/main/resources/rawfile/` and `entry/src/main/ets/common/svg/`, especially `svg-style-manifest.json` and `SvgStyleMap.ets`, before implementing icon-heavy screens.
10. Implement final UI from HTML/design-alignment, preserve the scaffold shell contract when present, apply prepared SVG assets and style metadata, enforce the SVG gate for rawfile icon usage, bind semantic IDs, run the final build, and fix failures until it passes.
</Implementation_Order>

<Progress_Tracking>
Track progress while implementing:

- When design alignment arrives during active headless work, append the visual integration/final build task at the end instead of inserting it ahead of the current coherent logic task.

<Local_References>

- `/Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id/references/uitree-to-arkts-mapping.md` when mapping semantic UI targets to ArkTS controls

1	# 今天吃什么 — 产品需求文档
2	
3	## 摘要
4	
5	一款面向日常饮食决策的 HarmonyOS 生活助手 App。用户在"不知道吃什么"时，可以通过随机转盘快速得到今日菜品推荐；也可以基于冰箱已有食材获得搭配建议；还能按心情、用餐时段进行筛选。完成用餐后可进行每日饮食打卡，并生成分享卡片记录生活。
6	
7	## 价值主张
8	
9	把"今天吃什么"从反复纠结变成一次有趣、有记录、可分享的决策体验。App 不仅提供即时答案，还帮助用户管理冰箱食材、追踪饮食习惯，并通过分享卡片让日常三餐变得更有仪式感。
10	
11	## 产品类别与主要用户场景
12	
13	- **产品类别**：生活工具 / 饮食决策助手
14	- **目标用户**：日常自己做饭或点外卖、希望减少选择困难的年轻用户和家庭用户
15	- **主要场景**：
16	  1. 临近饭点没有主意，打开 App 转转盘决定今天吃什么。
17	  2. 冰箱里还有剩余食材，输入后获得推荐菜品。
18	  3. 想吃点特别的，按心情或时间筛选后决定。
19	  4. 吃完后打卡记录，连续打卡养成饮食记录习惯。
20	  5. 生成精美分享卡片，分享到社交平台。
21	
22	## 核心任务（Jobs-to-be-Done）
23	
24	1. **快速决策**：通过随机转盘在一众菜品中抽出一个今日推荐，减少选择焦虑。
25	2. **食材利用**：录入冰箱现有食材，系统基于食材推荐可做的菜品，减少浪费。
26	3. **情境筛选**：根据当前心情或用餐时段快速过滤出合适的菜品列表。
27	4. **饮食打卡**：记录每日三餐内容，形成连续打卡日历和统计。
28	5. **社交分享**：将今日菜品或打卡记录生成分享卡片，便于保存或分享。
29	
30	## 页面结构
31	
32	| 页面 | 页面 ID | 说明 |
33	|------|---------|------|
34	| 首页 | `home-page` | 今日状态、快捷入口、最近打卡、转盘入口 |
35	| 转盘页 | `wheel-page` | 转盘抽菜、选择/编辑候选菜品、查看结果 |
36	| 食材推荐页 | `ingredient-page` | 管理冰箱食材、按食材推荐菜品 |
37	| 筛选页 | `filter-page` | 按心情/时段筛选菜品 |
38	| 打卡页 | `checkin-page` | 每日打卡、日历视图、打卡统计 |
39	| 分享页 | `share-page` | 生成并预览分享卡片 |
40	| 菜品管理页 | `dish-page` | 添加、编辑、删除个人菜品库 |
41	
42	## 导航模型
43	
44	底部 Tab 导航，包含 4 个一级入口：
45	
46	1. **首页**（tab-home）
47	2. **推荐**（tab-recommend）— 聚合转盘、食材、筛选三个入口
48	3. **打卡**（tab-checkin）
49	4. **我的**（tab-profile）— 包含菜品管理、设置
50	
51	推荐页内使用二级分段控制（Segment）切换：转盘 / 食材 / 筛选。
52	
53	## 数据来源策略
54	
55	- **本地持久化**：使用 App 本地 Preference / 关系型数据库（RDB）存储：
56	  - 用户自定义菜品库
57	  - 冰箱食材清单
58	  - 每日打卡记录
59	  - 转盘候选池设置
60	- **首次启动状态**：不预置默认种子数据。首屏显示空状态引导用户添加第一道菜品或食材。若后续需要演示截图，HTML 设计稿中可填充视觉样本数据，但这不是产品功能要求。
61	- **推荐算法**：基于本地规则匹配（食材包含度 + 心情/时段标签过滤），无需后端或网络。
62	- **分享卡片**：基于本地数据，通过 Canvas / 组件截图生成图片文件保存到相册。
63	
64	## 组件结构
65	
66	- **全局**
67	  - `BottomTabBar`：底部导航
68	  - `TopHeader`：页面标题与返回/操作按钮
69	  - `LoadingState` / `EmptyState` / `ErrorState`：通用状态组件
70	- **首页**
71	  - `TodayStatusCard`：今日已打卡状态与推荐入口
72	  - `QuickActionGrid`：转盘、食材、筛选快捷入口
73	  - `RecentCheckinList`：最近打卡摘要
74	- **转盘**
75	  - `SpinWheel`：可旋转转盘
76	  - `WheelResultPanel`：抽中结果展示
77	  - `CandidateChips`：候选菜品管理
78	- **推荐/食材**
79	  - `IngredientInput`：食材输入框
80	  - `IngredientTagList`：已添加食材标签
81	  - `DishRecommendationList`：推荐菜品列表
82	- **筛选**
83	  - `MoodFilterChips`：心情标签
84	  - `TimeFilterChips`：用餐时段标签
85	  - `FilteredDishGrid`：筛选结果网格
86	- **打卡**
87	  - `CheckinCalendar`：打卡日历
88	  - `MealTypeSelector`：早餐/午餐/晚餐
89	  - `CheckinForm`：打卡内容表单
90	  - `CheckinStatsPanel`：连续打卡/累计打卡统计
91	- **分享**
92	  - `ShareCardPreview`：分享卡片预览
93	  - `ShareActionBar`：保存/分享操作
94	- **菜品管理**
95	  - `DishList`：菜品列表
96	  - `DishFormSheet`：添加/编辑菜品表单
97	
98	## Target Device
99	
100	- 屏幕分辨率：1272vp × 2756vp（宽 × 高）
101	- 设备类型：普通手机（非折叠机、非平板）
102	- 像素密度倍率：3x（即 HTML 设计稿 390px = 设备上 1170vp 物理像素，但 ArkTS 使用 vp 单位时 1px≈1vp）
103	- 布局策略：内容区 max-width 约束在 390-420vp 内居中，禁止全宽铺满。**注意：Tab 导航栏等必须在底部，且若使用 `position: fixed` 必须限制宽度与 body 一致（如 `width: 100%; max-width: 390px; margin: 0 auto; left: 0; right: 0;`），严禁在 PC 浏览器上全宽拉伸。严禁生成在侧边（如右边/左边）**。
104	- 特殊注意：2756px 高度意味着屏幕很长，滚动区域充足，但需确保首屏关键内容（Header + 核心卡片）在顶部 600vp 内可见。
105	
106	## 验收标准
107	
108	- [ ] 用户能在转盘页添加候选菜品并点击转盘进行抽取，抽中结果明确展示。
109	- [ ] 用户能在食材页输入冰箱食材，系统基于食材推荐可做菜列表。
110	- [ ] 用户能按心情/时段筛选，获得匹配的菜品列表。
111	- [ ] 用户能在打卡页选择日期、餐别并记录内容，日历上正确显示打卡标记。
112	- [ ] 用户能在分享页生成包含今日菜品或打卡信息的卡片，并触发保存/分享。
113	- [ ] 用户能在菜品管理页增删改查个人菜品库。
114	- [ ] 所有数据在 App 生命周期内持久化，重启后仍然存在。
115	- [ ] 构建通过，App 能在目标设备上正常启动并运行。
116	
117	## 非目标（Out of Scope）
118	
119	- 不接入第三方菜谱 API 或网络搜索。
120	- 不提供社区、关注、点赞等社交功能。
121	- 不提供营养成分计算或热量追踪。
122	- 不预置默认菜品/食材种子数据。
123	
124	## 开放问题
125	
126	1. 是否需要为转盘添加音效或震动反馈？（建议作为后续增强）
127	2. 分享卡片是否包含二维码/小程序跳转？（建议首版不包含）
128	3. 菜品库是否支持从相册导入图片？（建议首版使用默认占位图或纯色卡片）
129	
130	---
131	
132	*文档版本：v1.0*  
133	*生成日期：2026/06/23*
134

# 今天吃什么 — 产品需求文档

## 摘要

一款面向日常饮食决策的 HarmonyOS 生活助手 App。用户在"不知道吃什么"时，可以通过随机转盘快速得到今日菜品推荐；也可以基于冰箱已有食材获得搭配建议；还能按心情、用餐时段进行筛选。完成用餐后可进行每日饮食打卡，并生成分享卡片记录生活。

## 价值主张

把"今天吃什么"从反复纠结变成一次有趣、有记录、可分享的决策体验。App 不仅提供即时答案，还帮助用户管理冰箱食材、追踪饮食习惯，并通过分享卡片让日常三餐变得更有仪式感。

## 产品类别与主要用户场景

- **产品类别**：生活工具 / 饮食决策助手
- **目标用户**：日常自己做饭或点外卖、希望减少选择困难的年轻用户和家庭用户
- **主要场景**：
  1. 临近饭点没有主意，打开 App 转转盘决定今天吃什么。
  2. 冰箱里还有剩余食材，输入后获得推荐菜品。
  3. 想吃点特别的，按心情或时间筛选后决定。
  4. 吃完后打卡记录，连续打卡养成饮食记录习惯。
  5. 生成精美分享卡片，分享到社交平台。

## 核心任务（Jobs-to-be-Done）

1. **快速决策**：通过随机转盘在一众菜品中抽出一个今日推荐，减少选择焦虑。
2. **食材利用**：录入冰箱现有食材，系统基于食材推荐可做的菜品，减少浪费。
3. **情境筛选**：根据当前心情或用餐时段快速过滤出合适的菜品列表。
4. **饮食打卡**：记录每日三餐内容，形成连续打卡日历和统计。
5. **社交分享**：将今日菜品或打卡记录生成分享卡片，便于保存或分享。

## 页面结构

| 页面 | 页面 ID | 说明 |
|------|---------|------|
| 首页 | `home-page` | 今日状态、快捷入口、最近打卡、转盘入口 |
| 转盘页 | `wheel-page` | 转盘抽菜、选择/编辑候选菜品、查看结果 |
| 食材推荐页 | `ingredient-page` | 管理冰箱食材、按食材推荐菜品 |
| 筛选页 | `filter-page` | 按心情/时段筛选菜品 |
| 打卡页 | `checkin-page` | 每日打卡、日历视图、打卡统计 |
| 分享页 | `share-page` | 生成并预览分享卡片 |
| 菜品管理页 | `dish-page` | 添加、编辑、删除个人菜品库 |

## 导航模型

底部 Tab 导航，包含 4 个一级入口：

1. **首页**（tab-home）
2. **推荐**（tab-recommend）— 聚合转盘、食材、筛选三个入口
3. **打卡**（tab-checkin）
4. **我的**（tab-profile）— 包含菜品管理、设置

推荐页内使用二级分段控制（Segment）切换：转盘 / 食材 / 筛选。

## 数据来源策略

- **本地持久化**：使用 App 本地 Preference / 关系型数据库（RDB）存储：
  - 用户自定义菜品库
  - 冰箱食材清单
  - 每日打卡记录
  - 转盘候选池设置
- **首次启动状态**：不预置默认种子数据。首屏显示空状态引导用户添加第一道菜品或食材。若后续需要演示截图，HTML 设计稿中可填充视觉样本数据，但这不是产品功能要求。
- **推荐算法**：基于本地规则匹配（食材包含度 + 心情/时段标签过滤），无需后端或网络。
- **分享卡片**：基于本地数据，通过 Canvas / 组件截图生成图片文件保存到相册。

## 组件结构

- **全局**
  - `BottomTabBar`：底部导航
  - `TopHeader`：页面标题与返回/操作按钮
  - `LoadingState` / `EmptyState` / `ErrorState`：通用状态组件
- **首页**
  - `TodayStatusCard`：今日已打卡状态与推荐入口
  - `QuickActionGrid`：转盘、食材、筛选快捷入口
  - `RecentCheckinList`：最近打卡摘要
- **转盘**
  - `SpinWheel`：可旋转转盘
  - `WheelResultPanel`：抽中结果展示
  - `CandidateChips`：候选菜品管理
- **推荐/食材**
  - `IngredientInput`：食材输入框
  - `IngredientTagList`：已添加食材标签
  - `DishRecommendationList`：推荐菜品列表
- **筛选**
  - `MoodFilterChips`：心情标签
  - `TimeFilterChips`：用餐时段标签
  - `FilteredDishGrid`：筛选结果网格
- **打卡**
  - `CheckinCalendar`：打卡日历
  - `MealTypeSelector`：早餐/午餐/晚餐
  - `CheckinForm`：打卡内容表单
  - `CheckinStatsPanel`：连续打卡/累计打卡统计
- **分享**
  - `ShareCardPreview`：分享卡片预览
  - `ShareActionBar`：保存/分享操作
- **菜品管理**
  - `DishList`：菜品列表
  - `DishFormSheet`：添加/编辑菜品表单

## Target Device

- 屏幕分辨率：1272vp × 2756vp（宽 × 高）
- 设备类型：普通手机（非折叠机、非平板）
- 像素密度倍率：3x（即 HTML 设计稿 390px = 设备上 1170vp 物理像素，但 ArkTS 使用 vp 单位时 1px≈1vp）
- 布局策略：内容区 max-width 约束在 390-420vp 内居中，禁止全宽铺满。**注意：Tab 导航栏等必须在底部，且若使用 `position: fixed` 必须限制宽度与 body 一致（如 `width: 100%; max-width: 390px; margin: 0 auto; left: 0; right: 0;`），严禁在 PC 浏览器上全宽拉伸。严禁生成在侧边（如右边/左边）**。
- 特殊注意：2756px 高度意味着屏幕很长，滚动区域充足，但需确保首屏关键内容（Header + 核心卡片）在顶部 600vp 内可见。

## 验收标准

- [ ] 用户能在转盘页添加候选菜品并点击转盘进行抽取，抽中结果明确展示。
- [ ] 用户能在食材页输入冰箱食材，系统基于食材推荐可做菜列表。
- [ ] 用户能按心情/时段筛选，获得匹配的菜品列表。
- [ ] 用户能在打卡页选择日期、餐别并记录内容，日历上正确显示打卡标记。
- [ ] 用户能在分享页生成包含今日菜品或打卡信息的卡片，并触发保存/分享。
- [ ] 用户能在菜品管理页增删改查个人菜品库。
- [ ] 所有数据在 App 生命周期内持久化，重启后仍然存在。
- [ ] 构建通过，App 能在目标设备上正常启动并运行。

## 非目标（Out of Scope）

- 不接入第三方菜谱 API 或网络搜索。
- 不提供社区、关注、点赞等社交功能。
- 不提供营养成分计算或热量追踪。
- 不预置默认菜品/食材种子数据。

## 开放问题

1. 是否需要为转盘添加音效或震动反馈？（建议作为后续增强）
2. 分享卡片是否包含二维码/小程序跳转？（建议首版不包含）
3. 菜品库是否支持从相册导入图片？（建议首版使用默认占位图或纯色卡片）

lastPrompt: /ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id ## UITREE DAG IMPLEMENTATION Original task: 为这个 HarmonyOS 工程实现一个‘今天吃什么’决策App，包含随机转盘抽菜、按冰箱食材推荐、按心情/时间筛选，以及每日饮食打卡记录和分享卡片功能，并完成构建和运行验证 Continue this implementa…

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me understand the structure:

**Tabs (4):**
- tab-home → home-page
- tab-recommend → recommend-page (segment-switched: wheel/ingredient/filter)
- tab-checkin → checkin-page
- tab-profile → profile-page (includes dish library)

**Non-tab surfaces (pushed):**
- wheel-page (parentTabId: tab-recommend)
- ingredient-page (parentTabId: tab-recommend)
- filter-page (parentTabId: tab-recommend)
- share-page
- dish-page

Wait, but the spec says "推荐页内使用二级分段控制（Segment）切换：转盘 / 食材 / 筛选" — so the recommend page uses segments. But the ui-tree also has wheel-page, ingredient-page, filter-page as separate surfaces with parentTabId: tab-recommend and routes pages/Wheel, pages/Ingredient, pages/Filter.

Hmm, there's some ambiguity. The spec says the recommend page contains wheel/ingredient/filter as segments (sub-tabs within recommend). But the ui-tree also has these as separate surfaces with routes. Let me reconcile:

- The home-page has buttons: navigate-to-wheel, navigate-to-ingredient, navigate-to-filter, navigate-to-checkin. These suggest full-page navigation to wheel/ingredient/filter.
- The recommend-page has segment targets switch-to-wheel/ingredient/filter.
- wheel-page/ingredient-page/filter-page have parentTabId: tab-recommend, suggesting they are "within" recommend but as separate surfaces.

So the design: The recommend tab page contains a segment control that switches between wheel/ingredient/filter panels. But each panel is substantial enough to also be its own surface with semantic targets. The home page's quick actions (home-spin-wheel-button etc.) navigate to recommend tab and select the appropriate segment.

Actually, looking more carefully: home buttons emit navigate-to-wheel etc. — these should switch to recommend tab + the matching segment. And the wheel/ingredient/filter surfaces are the panel content within recommend.

Given the existing shell pattern (Index.ets with AppSurface), I'll model it as:
- Tab surfaces: home, recommend, checkin, profile
- Within recommend, a local segment state switches the displayed panel (wheel/ingredient/filter)
- Pushed/non-tab surfaces: share, dish-library (and dish-form is a sheet)

Wait but wheel/ingredient/filter have parentTabId not tabId. So they're not standalone tabs. They are surfaces nested under recommend. I'll implement them as panels switched by segment within the Recommend page, but each panel = a full component file. That keeps it simple and matches the spec's segment approach.

Actually, to keep the Index shell simple, I'll treat the recommend tab as rendering the RecommendPage which internally has the segment + 3 panels. The home quick-action buttons call goToTab('tab-recommend') + set the recommend segment. share and dish-library are non-tab pushed surfaces in Index.

Let me design the architecture:

**Models (model/):**
- `Dish.ets` — Dish class: id, name, tags (string[]), ingredients (string[]), moods (string[]), times (string[])
- `Ingredient.ets` — could be just string, but make a small model
- `Checkin.ets` — Checkin class: id, dateKey (YYYY-MM-DD), meal (breakfast/lunch/dinner), dishName, note, createdAt
- `Enums.ets` — MealType, WheelState, Mood, TimeSlot enums

**Services (services/):**
- `Storage.ets` — a Preferences-backed storage helper (DataStore)
- `DishService.ets` — CRUD over dish library
- `CheckinService.ets` — CRUD over checkins, streak/total computation
- `IngredientService.ets` — manage fridge ingredients
- `RecommendService.ets` — recommendation logic (ingredient match + mood/time filter)
- `ShareService.ets` — generate card data + save image to album (stub/Canvas)

**Controllers (controller/):**
Per the existing pattern there's a controller dir. But for ArkUI, state management with AppStorage / @State in pages is more idiomatic. I'll create a single `AppStore` using AppStorage for shared state, and controllers per feature.

Actually given the headless-first approach, let me create controllers that hold logic and expose state/event methods. Then pages wire to them.

Let me think about persistence. HarmonyOS Preferences API: `@kit.ArkData` → `preferences`. Use `preferences.getPreferences(context, 'name')`. But context is needed. A common pattern: store a global context in EntryAbility onWindowStageCreate. I'll create a Storage service that takes context.

For simplicity and ArkTS-strict compatibility, I'll use AppStorage for in-memory state + Preferences for persistence, with a StorageService that lazily loads on init.

Given the memory note about ArkTS strict build gotchas (spreads/indexed-access/builder-chaining/shape-API), I need to be careful:
- No spread operators in object literals for class instances
- Avoid `someObject[key]` indexed access on typed objects
- Careful with builder chaining

Let me keep it pragmatic. For the headless logic phase, I'll implement:
1. Models/Enums
2. Storage service (Preferences wrapper)
3. Domain services (Dish, Checkin, Ingredient, Recommend)
4. Controllers that orchestrate

Then pages for visual integration. Since the skill says implement headless logic first until design artifacts ready — but there are no HTML design artifacts yet (design_exports empty, no page-*.html). So I should implement headless logic first AND check static. But wait — the original task asks "并完成构建和运行验证" (complete build and runtime verification). And the completion criteria for visual integration requires a passing build.

However, the skill's Execution_Flow says: when visual artifacts are not available, implement headless logic and avoid committing to final layout, run CheckArktsFiles and stop. content.content:
<system-reminder>This memory is 2 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	---
2	name: arkts-strict-build-gotchas
3	description: "Non-obvious ArkTS strict-mode rules that break HarmonyOS builds in this repo (spreads, indexed access, builder chaining, shape APIs)"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: reference
7	  originSessionId: 6b634090-75b4-47ff-86cf-bff9305587e3
8	---
9	
10	ArkTS strict mode (this repo, SDK 6.0.2(22), `useNormalizedOHMUrl`) rejects several common patterns. Hit while building 平安扣 Pinankou; each caused a hard compile error:
11	
12	- **No spreads** (`arkts-no-spread`): `[h, ...arr]` and `{...obj}` both rejected. Build arrays with `push`/loops; construct objects field-by-field. (`Math.max(...nums)` also risky — loop instead.)
13	- **No indexed access on object/interface fields** (`arkts-no-props-by-index`): `RISK_LABEL[level]` on an interface-typed const is illegal. Use a `switch`/`if` function (`riskLabel(level)`).
14	- **No indexed access types** (`arkts-no-aliases-by-index`): `HazardProgress['type']` illegal — define a named `type ProgressType = ...`.
15	- **No utility types** (`arkts-no-utility-types`): `Omit<T,'id'>` illegal — define an explicit interface.
16	- **No untyped object literals** (`arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals`): arrow `(n,i) => ({...})` needs explicit return type; `JSON.parse(x)` needs `as PersistInterface`.
17	- **No inline object-literal types** (`arkts-no-obj-literals-as-types`): `(p: {label:string}) =>` illegal — use a named interface. This also forbids **unions of object-literal types** as a result type, e.g. `type Outcome = {ok:true; x:T} | {ok:false; reason:string}`. Define each arm as a named `interface` (`BookingSuccess`/`BookingFailure`) then `type Outcome = BookingSuccess | BookingFailure`. Without a named interface, returned `{ok:false,...}` literals also trip `arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals` (no contextual type).
18	- **Don't chain universal attrs after a builder-param custom component**: `SectionCard(){...}.margin(...)` is a parse error (`;` expected). Add a `@Prop marginTop` and apply `.margin` inside the component instead. `.id()`/`.margin()` on a plain custom component (no @BuilderParam) IS fine.
19	- **Don't chain attrs on a @Builder method call**: `this.MyBuilder(...).id('x')` → `id does not exist on type void`. Pass the id as a builder param and bind it on the root element inside.
20	- **Reserved member names**: a @Prop/callback named `onClick`, `enabled`, `size`, `onChange`, etc. collides with CustomComponent universal attrs. Rename (`onTap`, `isEnabled`, `ringSize`, `onTextChange`).
21	- **Shape API specifics**: `Line.startPoint([x,y])` / `endPoint([x,y])` take **arrays**, not `{x,y}`. `Polyline.points([[x,y],...])`. Line cap is `.strokeLineCap(LineCapStyle.Round)` (not `strokeCap`).
22	- **Phone call**: `import { call } from '@kit.TelephonyKit'` (NOT AbilityKit); `call.makeCall(phone: string): Promise<void>`. Note `call.makeCall` needs system permission `ohos.permission.PLACE_CALL` — for third-party apps prefer launching the dialer via `Want { action: 'ohos.want.action.dial', uri: 'tel:...' }` + `context.startAbility(want)`.
23	- **`AppStorage`/`PersistentStorage`/`LocalStorage` are globals, NOT imports**: `import { AppStorage } from '@kit.ArkUI'` → hard error (2305 "no exported member"). Use them bare, no import line.
24	- **`this` is illegal inside `static` methods** (`arkts-no-standalone-this`, code 93): even in `static init()` you cannot write `this.initialized`. Reference via the class name: `Persistence.initialized`.
25	- **`startAbility` is on `UIAbilityContext`/`AbilityContext`, not base `Context`** (2339): if you stash an ability context for later `startAbility`, type it as `common.UIAbilityContext` (what `UIAbility.this.context` is), not `common.Context`.
26

  originSessionId: 6b634090-75b4-47ff-86cf-bff9305587e3
---

ArkTS strict mode (this repo, SDK 6.0.2(22), `useNormalizedOHMUrl`) rejects several common patterns. Hit while building 平安扣 Pinankou; each caused a hard compile error:

- **No spreads** (`arkts-no-spread`): `[h, ...arr]` and `{...obj}` both rejected. Build arrays with `push`/loops; construct objects field-by-field. (`Math.max(...nums)` also risky — loop instead.)
- **No indexed access on object/interface fields** (`arkts-no-props-by-index`): `RISK_LABEL[level]` on an interface-typed const is illegal. Use a `switch`/`if` function (`riskLabel(level)`).
- **No indexed access types** (`arkts-no-aliases-by-index`): `HazardProgress['type']` illegal — define a named `type ProgressType = ...`.
- **No utility types** (`arkts-no-utility-types`): `Omit<T,'id'>` illegal — define an explicit interface.
- **No untyped object literals** (`arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals`): arrow `(n,i) => ({...})` needs explicit return type; `JSON.parse(x)` needs `as PersistInterface`.
- **No inline object-literal types** (`arkts-no-obj-literals-as-types`): `(p: {label:string}) =>` illegal — use a named interface. This also forbids **unions of object-literal types** as a result type, e.g. `type Outcome = {ok:true; x:T} | {ok:false; reason:string}`. Define each arm as a named `interface` (`BookingSuccess`/`BookingFailure`) then `type Outcome = BookingSuccess | BookingFailure`. Without a named interface, returned `{ok:false,...}` literals also trip `arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals` (no contextual type).
- **Don't chain universal attrs after a builder-param custom component**: `SectionCard(){...}.margin(...)` is a parse error (`;` expected). Add a `@Prop marginTop` and apply `.margin` inside the component instead. `.id()`/`.margin()` on a plain custom component (no @BuilderParam) IS fine.
- **Don't chain attrs on a @Builder method call**: `this.MyBuilder(...).id('x')` → `id does not exist on type void`. Pass the id as a builder param and bind it on the root element inside.
- **Reserved member names**: a @Prop/callback named `onClick`, `enabled`, `size`, `onChange`, etc. collides with CustomComponent universal attrs. Rename (`onTap`, `isEnabled`, `ringSize`, `onTextChange`).
- **Shape API specifics**: `Line.startPoint([x,y])` / `endPoint([x,y])` take **arrays**, not `{x,y}`. `Polyline.points([[x,y],...])`. Line cap is `.strokeLineCap(LineCapStyle.Round)` (not `strokeCap`).
- **Phone call**: `import { call } from '@kit.TelephonyKit'` (NOT AbilityKit); `call.makeCall(phone: string): Promise<void>`. Note `call.makeCall` needs system permission `ohos.permission.PLACE_CALL` — for third-party apps prefer launching the dialer via `Want { action: 'ohos.want.action.dial', uri: 'tel:...' }` + `context.startAbility(want)`.
- **`AppStorage`/`PersistentStorage`/`LocalStorage` are globals, NOT imports**: `import { AppStorage } from '@kit.ArkUI'` → hard error (2305 "no exported member"). Use them bare, no import line.
- **`this` is illegal inside `static` methods** (`arkts-no-standalone-this`, code 93): even in `static init()` you cannot write `this.initialized`. Reference via the class name: `Persistence.initialized`.
- **`startAbility` is on `UIAbilityContext`/`AbilityContext`, not base `Context`** (2339): if you stash an ability context for later `startAbility`, type it as `common.UIAbilityContext` (what `UIAbility.this.context` is), not `common.Context`.

Very useful. Key rules to follow:
- No spreads — build arrays with push/loops
- No indexed access on object fields — use switch/if
- No untyped object literals — explicit interfaces; JSON.parse needs `as`
- No unions of object-literal types — name each interface
- AppStorage/PersistentStorage are globals, NOT imports
- No `this` in static methods
- Don't chain attrs after builder-param custom components / builder method calls
- Reserved member names collide

Also for `@Observed`/persistence: I'll use plain classes + AppStorage for global keys.

content.thinking:
Good — I understand the format. I'll author my own simpler icons. Since these are hand-authored (not extracted from HTML), I'll keep fill-mode icons with baked colors. I'll author:

Tab icons (need on/off): home, recommend, checkin, profile
Action icons: spin, add, back, search, trash, share, save, settings, close, chevron

Let me create fill-mode SVGs with baked colors. The SVG gate concerns: paint_mode "stroke" or empty fill_color → never chain fillColor(). My icons are fill-mode with fill_color set, so even if I don't call fillColor (I won't), they render baked colors. Good. I'll NOT call fillColor anywhere.

For active/inactive tabs, I bake different colors into separate files.

Let me create the rawfile directory and SVGs. I'll write each SVG file. Colors:
- active green: #006F45
- inactive: #B7AC9D
- action dark: #0F3329
- action green: #006F45

Let me design simple, clean 24x24 fill icons.

home: house shape. Path: a roof + body. Fill.
A simple house: `M12 3 2 11h3v9h5v-6h4v6h5v-9h3z` roughly. Let me craft a recognizable one.

Actually simpler robust approach: use basic shapes. But path is fine.

Let me write each SVG. I'll keep them clean.

home-on/home-off: same path, different fill.
`<svg xmlns viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M12 3 2.5 11h2v9h5v-6h5v6h5v-9h2z"/></svg>` — a house. Let me refine coordinates: viewbox 0 0 24 24. Roof peak at (12,2). Left base (2,11), right base (22,11). Walls: left wall x4..10, right x14..20. Let me just use a standard house path:
`M12 3l9 8h-2v9h-5v-6h-4v6H5v-9H3z`

recommend (sparkle): a 4-point star/sparkle. `M12 2l2.4 6.6L21 11l-6.6 2.4L12 20l-2.4-6.6L3 11l6.6-2.4z` — diamond star. Good for "recommend/discover".

checkin (checkmark in circle): circle + check. Two elements: `<circle cx=12 cy=12 r=10 fill=COLOR/><path fill=#fff d="M10.2 15.3l-3-3 1.4-1.4 1.6 1.6 4.4-4.4 1.4 1.4z"/>` 

profile (person): head circle + shoulders. `<circle cx=12 cy=8 r=4/><path d="M4 20c0-4 4-6 8-6s8 2 8 6z"/>` — but path + circle both fill. Combine: two elements.

spin (refresh/circular arrow): tricky in fill. Use a near-circle with arrowhead. Simpler: a circular arrow path. Let me use:
`M12 4V1L8 5l4 4V6a6 6 0 1 1-6 6H4a8 8 0 1 0 8-8z` — refresh icon. Good.

add (plus): `M11 5h2v6h6v2h-6v6h-2v-6H5v-2h6z` — plus sign fill. Or thicker.

back (chevron-left): `M15.5 4 8 12l7.5 8 1.4-1.4L10.8 12l6.1-6.6z` 

search: `<path d="M10 2a8 8 0 1 0 4.9 14.3l5.4 5.4 1.4-1.4-5.4-5.4A8 8 0 0 0 10 2zm0 2a6 6 0 1 1 0 12 6 6 0 0 1 0-12z"/>`

trash: `M9 3h6l1 2h4v2H4V5h4zM6 8h12l-1 12H7z` — approximate trash. Let me use a cleaner one.

Actually, let me reduce icon count. Trash is used in dish delete + candidate clear. I'll keep it.

Let me simplify trash: `<path d="M9 2h6v2h5v2H4V4h5zm-2.5 6h11l-.8 12.5a2 2 0 0 1-2 1.5H9.3a2 2 0 0 1-2-1.5z"/>`

share: `<path d="M14 2a3 3 0 1 1-2.8 4l-5 2.6a3 3 0 1 1 0 2.8l5 2.6A3 3 0 1 1 14 16l-5-2.6a3 3 0 0 0 0-2.8L14 8A3 3 0 0 1 11 5z"/>` — no, that's wrong. Share icon: three dots connected. Path is complex. Let me use a simpler "share" = upward arrow out of a box (share/upload):
`<path d="M12 2l5 5h-3v7h-4V7H7zM4 14h4v2H6v4h12v-4h-2v-2h4v8H4z"/>` — upload/share arrow. Good enough (save-to-share semantics).

Hmm "save" and "share" both arrows. Let me differentiate:
- save (download to album): down arrow into tray: `<path d="M12 3l5 5h-3v6h-4V8H7zM5 18h14v2H5z"/>` no. Download icon: arrow down + tray: `M11 3h2v9l3.3-3.3 1.4 1.4L12 16.8 6.3 10.1l1.4-1.4L11 12zM5 19h14v2H5z` → `M11 4h2v8l3-3 1.4 1.4L12 16.2 6.6 10.4 8 9l3 3zM5 18h14v2H5z`
- share: nodes (three-circle). Let me just do: `<path d="M16 2a3 3 0 1 1-2.6 4.5l-4.5 2.4A3 3 0 1 1 6 14l4.5 2.4A3 3 0 1 1 13 18l-4.5-2.4A3 3 0 1 1 4 10l4.5-2.4A3 3 0 0 1 11 5a3 3 0 0 1 5-3z"/>` — too complex/messy.

Let me use simpler distinct metaphors:
- share = box with up arrow (send): `<path d="M4 12 12 4l8 8h-5v8h-6v-8z"/>` (up arrow). 
- save = camera/image-ish for album save. Use a photo frame: `<path d="M4 5h16v14H4zm2 2v8l3.5-3.5L12 14l3-3.5L18 14V7z M8.5 10.5a1.5 1.5 0 1 0 0-3 1.5 1.5 0 0 0 0 3z"/>` (image/photo). Good for "save to album".

mkdir -p entry/src/main/resources/rawfile
DIR=entry/src/main/resources/rawfile

# Colors
ACTIVE=#006F45
INACTIVE=#B7AC9D
DARK=#0F3329
GREEN=#006F45
WHITE=#FFFFFF

write_svg() { printf '%s' "$2" > "$DIR/$1"; }

# --- Tabs: home ---
H='<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M12 3 2.5 11.2h2.1V21h5.1v-6.3h4.6V21h5.1v-9.8h2.1z"/></svg>'
write_svg "home-on.svg" "${H/COLOR/$ACTIVE}"
write_svg "home-off.svg" "${H/COLOR/$INACTIVE}"

# --- Tabs: recommend (sparkle/diamond star) ---
S='<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M12 2l2.3 6.7L21 11l-6.7 2.3L12 20l-2.3-6.7L3 11l6.7-2.3z"/></svg>'
write_svg "recommend-on.svg" "${S/COLOR/$ACTIVE}"
write_svg "recommend-off.svg" "${S/COLOR/$INACTIVE}"

# --- Tabs: checkin (check in circle) ---

write_svg "checkin-on.svg" "${C/COLOR/$ACTIVE}"
write_svg "checkin-off.svg" "${C/COLOR/$INACTIVE}"

# --- Tabs: profile (person) ---
P='<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M12 3a4 4 0 1 1 0 8 4 4 0 0 1 0-8zM4 20c0-3.6 3.6-6 8-6s8 2.4 8 6v1H4z"/></svg>'
write_svg "profile-on.svg" "${P/COLOR/$ACTIVE}"
write_svg "profile-off.svg" "${P/COLOR/$INACTIVE}"

# --- Actions ---
SP='<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M20 11a8 8 0 1 0-2.3 5.7l1.4 1.4A10 10 0 1 1 20 11zm-1-7v5h-5L16 7h-3a6 6 0 1 0 3 9l1.6 1.2A8 8 0 1 1 13 5h3z"/></svg>'

write_svg "search.svg" "${SEARCH/COLOR/$DARK}"

TRASH='<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="COLOR" d="M9 2h6v2h5v2H4V4h5zm-1.5 6h9l-.8 11.2A2 2 0 0 1 13.7 21H10.3a2 2 0 0 1-2-1.8z"/></svg>'

The prepared icons are mostly `paint_mode: stroke`. Per the SVG gate: for stroke-mode or empty fill_color, NEVER chain `.fillColor()` on `Image($rawfile(file_name))`. For selected/unselected/dynamic states, switch between prepared rawfile variants instead of tinting.

So my SvgIcon wrapper must NOT use fillColor at all for these. It just renders `Image($rawfile(name))`. The prepared SVGs bake their colors. For active/inactive tab states, there must be variants (e.g., icon_Home-Tab-1.svg vs icon_Home-Tab-2.svg, icon_Recommend-Tab-1.svg vs 2). content.subject: Create ui-behavior.json behavior contract
content.description: Write .arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json from spec.md + ui-tree.json: dataModel, services, stateMachine, interactionFlows, testTargets.

content.subject: Implement headless logic (models, enums, services, store)
content.description: Enums, Dish, Checkin models; Storage/Preferences wrapper; Dish/Checkin/Ingredient/Recommend/Share services; central Store singleton.

content.subject: Author rawfile SVG icons + manifest + SvgStyleMap
content.description: Create rawfile SVG icons (tabs active/inactive + actions), svg-style-manifest.json, common/svg/SvgStyleMap.ets honoring SVG gate.

content.subject: Update shell (TabTypes, Theme, SvgIcon, BottomTabBar, EntryAbility)
content.description: Theme tokens, TabTypes union, SvgIcon variant map, AppConstants; BottomTabBar tabs; update EntryAbility to init Store.

content.subject: Implement functional UI pages + components
content.description: Home, Recommend(wheel/ingredient/filter), Checkin, Profile, Share, DishLibrary pages + SpinWheel + EmptyState components. Bind semantic IDs from ui-tree.json.

content.subject: Update Index shell surface switching
content.description: Rewrite Index.ets surface model: tab surfaces home/recommend/checkin/profile; pushed share/dish; recommend segment; pending wheel result.

content.subject: Static check + build green
content.description: CheckArktsFiles on changed .ets, then build_project until green.

content.thinking:
I now have the design language. Key palette:
- paper #FFFCFA (page bg)
- cream #FFF5ED, vanilla #F8EFE6
- dark #2E2A25
- warm-gray #7A746E
- light-gray #F5F0EB
- border #E9E3DD
- orange #FF8C42 (brand), orange-deep #FF7043, orange-dark #E86A2E
- green #4ECB9C, olive #5A6B58, sage #8FA897
- tab inactive #A8A29C

The icons tab mapping:
- home active = icon_Home-Tab-2.svg (orange), inactive = icon_Home-Tab-1.svg (gray)
- recommend active = icon_Recommend-Tab-2.svg, inactive = icon_Recommend-Tab-1.svg
- checkin active = icon_Check-in-Tab-1.svg (orange), inactive = icon_Check-in-Tab-2.svg (gray)
- profile = icon_Profile-Tab.svg (gray) — only one; for active, no orange variant. I'll use it for both (acceptable).

Note home HTML has both today-card (with home-spin-wheel-button) and quick-grid cards (also home-spin-wheel-button duplicated, plus ingredient/filter/checkin). So home has 4 quick action cards.

Now I have enough design context. The remaining 3 HTML pages (recommend, ingredient, filter) I should read too, but given context size, let me read them to get the wheel/segment + ingredient + filter layouts. Let me read all three now.

Actually, the recommend page is the most complex (wheel + segments). Let me read recommend + ingredient + filter.

content.thinking:
I now have the full design language and all 4 designed pages. Key observations:
- Mood options in design: 开心, 想吃辣, 清淡, 治愈, 放纵, 随便 (6 moods) — differs slightly from my Enums (开心/放松/疲惫/馋辣/想清淡/赶时间). I should align Enums to the design's moods: ['开心', '馋辣', '清淡', '治愈', '放纵', '随便']. Let me update MOOD_OPTIONS to match design.
- Time slots: 早餐, 午餐, 晚餐, 夜宵 (4) — matches my TIME_SLOT_OPTIONS.
- Filter chips use a dark `active` background (#2E2A25) with white text/icon.
- Segment control (recommend): active = dark bg, white text.
- Wheel: 12-sector SVG with colors [orange, green, sage, cream]×3. Canvas in ArkTS — I'll draw sectors on a Canvas with rotation animation, or render the SVG image rotated. Simpler: use the prepared icon_Spinning-Wheel.svg as the wheel face and rotate the Image via animateTo on rotate angle. But that's a static 12-sector image — perfect, just rotate it. The pointer (icon_Wheel-Pointer.svg) stays fixed at top. On spin, animate rotation by N degrees, then pick result based on final angle / number of candidates.
- Tab bar: 4 tabs. Active has orange icon + label + dot.

Now, the recommendation/filter needs the dish library to have moods/times populated. Since no seed data, on first launch everything is empty. The filter/ingredient results will be empty until user adds dishes. That's per spec (no seed data). I'll show empty states.

Now let me reconcile the mood enum. I'll update MOOD_OPTIONS to the design's set. Let me update Enums.ets MOOD_OPTIONS to ['开心', '馋辣', '清淡', '治愈', '放纵', '随便'].

Now I have a lot to build. Given the scale, I'll delegate the bulk page implementation to an executor agent with precise instructions, OR continue solo. Given I've internalized all the design details, strict-mode rules, SVG gate, and Store API, doing it solo avoids re-explaining. But it's a LOT of code for my context.

Let me be pragmatic and efficient: I'll write the shared infrastructure myself (Theme tokens, TabTypes, SvgIcon, BottomTabBar, EntryAbility, AppController/AppStorage helpers, a reusable Chip/Card UI), then write each page. I'll keep pages focused.

Actually, to manage context and parallelize, let me delegate the page implementation to a single executor agent (opus) with a comprehensive spec, since pages are independent files sharing a well-defined contract (Store API + Theme + SvgIcon). I'll provide the exact contract. This is the right delegation per CLAUDE.md (route code to executor, opus for complex).

But risk: the executor may produce code that doesn't compile under strict mode or violates SVG gate. I'll give explicit guardrails. And I'll do the build myself (leader owns build).

Let me decide: I'll do the shell + shared components myself (high coupling, defines contract), then delegate the 6 page files to an executor with the contract locked. Actually the pages reference Store heavily and define the UI; they're the bulk. Let me delegate pages to executor after I've finalized the shell + a shared UI kit.

Hmm, but coordinating one big agent for all pages is fine. Let me write:
1. Theme (update tokens to design palette)
2. TabTypes (new TabId union)
3. SvgIcon (variant → icon_*.svg mapping, NO fillColor)
4. AppConstants (moods/times/meal labels, colors for non-token uses)
5. BottomTabBar (4 tabs with variant icons + dot)
6. EntryAbility (init Store)
7. Shared UI components: SectionCard, Chip, EmptyState, GradientButton (to reduce page boilerplate)
8. Index (surface switching)
9. Pages: Home, Recommend (wheel/ingredient/filter inline panels), Checkin, Profile, Share, DishLibrary

That's a lot. Let me delegate pages 9 to an executor while I do 1-8. Actually dependencies: pages depend on Theme/SvgIcon/AppConstants/components/Store. So I must finalize 1-8 first, then pages.

Given the coupling and that I've absorbed all context, and to avoid agent-communication overhead/strict-mode failures, I'll write everything myself but keep each file tight. Let me proceed sequentially but efficiently. I'll start with the shell infrastructure.

Let me first update MOOD_OPTIONS in Enums to match design.

Design moods: 开心, 想吃辣(→馋辣? design label is "想吃辣"), 清淡, 治愈, 放纵, 随便. Let me use exactly: ['开心', '想吃辣', '清淡', '治愈', '放纵', '随便'].

Let me update Enums.ets.

content.thinking:
Now a shared UI kit to reduce page boilerplate: GradientButton, Chip (selectable), SectionCard, EmptyState. I'll create one components file `UiKit.ets` exporting multiple structs? ArkTS: each @Component struct in a file is fine; multiple exports per file allowed. Let me make `components/UiKit.ets` with GradientButton, AppChip, EmptyState, and an icon swatch. Actually to keep each component clean and avoid reserved-name issues, let me create:
- `components/GradientButton.ets`
- `components/AppChip.ets`
- `components/EmptyState.ets`

But that's more files. Let me combine the small ones into `components/UiKit.ets`.

Components needed across pages:
1. GradientButton — orange gradient button, props: label, onTap, optional `flex`/width.
2. SelectableChip — for filter chips; props: label, iconVariant, selected, onTap. Active = dark bg + white icon/text. Per SVG gate: chip icon is stroke-mode; in active state the icon (white stroke) vs inactive (gray stroke). But prepared icons have fixed stroke colors (gray #7A746E or white). The design uses `currentColor` so chip icon color follows text color. But our prepared SVGs have BAKED colors, not currentColor. Hmm. 

Wait — the prepared SVGs were extracted with stroke_color values like #7A746E (mood chips). For active state the design shows white icons. But our SVGs are baked gray/white. For filter chips, the inactive chip should show gray icon, active shows white icon. The prepared mood icons are baked #7A746E (gray). There's no white-variant for mood icons except icon_Happy-Mood (white) and the chip active uses white.

Actually looking at manifest: icon_Happy-Mood.svg stroke=white, others (Spicy/Light/Comfort/Indulgent/Whatever) stroke=#7A746E. And icon_Breakfast-Time stroke=white. Inconsistent. This is because the design's chips use currentColor and the extractor captured whatever color appeared in the specific HTML instance.

This is the SVG-gate reality: I cannot tint. So for chips, the icon color won't perfectly match active/inactive. Options:
(a) Don't show icons in chips, just text (cleaner, avoids mismatch). The design shows icons but they're decorative; text-only chips are acceptable and avoid the tinting problem.
(b) Show the baked-color icon regardless.

Given the SVG gate explicitly says don't tint stroke icons, and the prepared icons have inconsistent baked colors, the cleanest compliant choice is text-only chips. But that loses some visual fidelity. 

Compromise: I'll render chips with the icon when available but accept the baked color; OR text-only. To stay visually clean AND gate-compliant, I'll make chips text-only with the dark-active style from the design. This is a reasonable, defensible choice. Actually, let me include icons using the gray-baked variants for inactive and... no. Text-only is cleanest. I'll do text-only selectable chips. Document this in handoff.

Hmm, but the skill says "Do not invent decorative icons when HTML does not define them" — the HTML DOES define chip icons, so dropping them is a minor fidelity reduction, not invention. Acceptable given gate constraints. I'll note it.

Actually, let me reconsider: I could show the chip icon using the prepared SVG (baked color) and it'll look fine in inactive state (gray icon on light bg) and in active state the icon stays its baked color on dark bg — a gray icon on dark bg is low-contrast. That's bad. So text-only is better. Decision: text-only chips.

3. EmptyState — props: title, desc, optional iconVariant.
4. SectionCard — a white rounded card with shadow + padding. Wrapper Column.

Let me also need a Toast feedback mechanism. ArkUI promptAction.showToast. I'll use `promptAction` from @kit.ArkUI in pages directly, or a helper. I'll add a `toast(msg)` helper in a `common/Feedback.ets`.

Let me create UiKit.ets + Feedback.ets.

Actually reserved name concern: a @Prop named `selected`, `label`, etc. are fine. `onTap` fine. Avoid `onClick`.

Let me write UiKit.ets with GradientButton, AppChip (text-only selectable), EmptyState, CardSection.

For GradientButton gradient: use `.linearGradient(BrandGradient.orange)` or backgroundEffect. ArkTS: `.linearGradient({angle,direction,colors})`. I'll apply directly.

Let me write it.

content.thinking:
All pages and components are written. Note: in DishLibraryPage, `ToggleArr` mutates `this.formMoods`/`this.formTimes` via reassignment — good for reactivity.

Also concern: `ToggleArr` takes `arr: string[]` param but I reassign `this.formMoods = next`, not the param. The param `arr` is unused effectively (I read it). Actually I iterate `arr` to build next, then assign to this.formMoods/times. That works. But I pass `this.formMoods` as arr — reading current value. Good.

Now let me mark task 5 complete and move to static check + build. Before building, let me run CheckArktsFiles on all the new .ets files to catch errors before the expensive build.

Let me also double check a few risky patterns:
1. HomePage: `this.FeatureCard({ item: {...} })` — passing object literal to @Builder. The FeatureItem interface is named. Should be OK. But the arrow `onTap: (): void => this.goToWheel()` inside object literal assigned to FeatureItem field — fine.

2. UiKit: `.linearGradient(BrandGradient.orange)` on Text — linearGradient applies to any component. OK.

3. `.opacity(0.9)` on Text — fine.

4. Grid usage in CheckinPage and FilterPanel — Grid needs columnsTemplate and the items flow. For CheckinPage calendar Grid with columnsTemplate 7 cols, items include weekday(7) + blanks + days. Grid auto-flows. Each GridItem contains a builder. OK. But Grid also needs a height or it may not render. Grid without explicit height in a Column — might collapse. Let me check: Grid typically needs height. In CheckinPage the calendar is inside a card Column; Grid without height may render with 0 height. I should set rowsGap and let it auto-size? Grid doesn't auto-size by content well. Hmm. This could be a rendering issue (not compile error). Let me set the Grid to use a reasonable approach: actually Grid will size to its children if I don't constrain, but often needs height. 

To be safe for calendar, maybe better to NOT use Grid and instead use Flex wrap with fixed-width cells. But 7-col calendar with Flex wrap would wrap naturally to 7 per row if each cell is width/7. Hard to guarantee. 

Alternative: use multiple Rows. But simpler: Grid with `rowsTemplate`? No. Let me set the Grid height to a computed value based on rows. Calendar has (1 weekday row + ceil((offset+days)/7) rows). Hard to compute statically.

Actually, ArkUI Grid does auto-layout to content height when no height set IF the parent allows. In a Column (which sizes to content), Grid should expand. But empirically Grid often needs an explicit height. To avoid risk, let me switch the calendar to a Flex wrap with cells of equal flex. I'll wrap cells in a Flex with wrap and give each cell `layoutWeight`? Flex doesn't do columnsTemplate. 

Simplest robust: render the calendar as successive Rows of 7. I'll precompute rows of 7 cells in buildCalendar and render with ForEach over rows, each a Row of 7. That's deterministic and avoids Grid height issues. Let me refactor CheckinPage calendar to rows.

Actually, that's a bigger refactor. Let me instead test with Grid first via the static check + build; if Grid renders, fine. But static check won't catch runtime layout. The build will succeed regardless. For runtime correctness, I'd need to launch (which is QA phase, not this skill). The skill says implement + build green; runtime QA is separate. So Grid compiling is enough for this skill's build gate. But I want the app actually functional.

Given the skill's scope (build green), and that runtime calendar layout is a QA concern, I'll keep Grid but add a reasonable height to be safe. Actually let me just refactor to rows to be safe since it's not much code and guarantees rendering. Let me do it — change buildCalendar to produce rows, and render with nested ForEach.

Hmm, that complicates. Let me instead keep Grid but it's known that ArkUI Grid auto-sizes when inside a scrollable/content column in many cases. I'll leave Grid and address in QA if needed. The build is the gate here.

Actually, I realize the CheckinPage root is a Scroll containing a Column. Grid inside should expand. I'll trust it. If QA shows issues, fix later.

content.thinking:
Build failed but ONLY on HomePage.ets — the Theme tokens resolved fine (so the earlier LSP errors were stale). The real error: HomePage's `this.FeatureCard({ item: {...} })` pattern — @Builder methods in ArkTS don't accept object-literal arguments the way I used. The error "Object literal must correspond to some explicitly declared class or interface (arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals)" and "'item' does not exist in type 'FeatureItem'".

The issue: passing `{ item: {...} }` to a @Builder — ArkTS @Builder invocation uses the parameter name as a property wrapper, but object literals inside need explicit typing. The cleaner fix: change FeatureCard @Builder to take individual params OR pass the FeatureItem directly without the `item:` wrapper. But the nested object literal `{ icon:..., onTap:... }` also trips arkts-no-untyped-obj-literals.

The cleanest fix: avoid the builder-object-literal pattern entirely. Replace the 4 FeatureCard calls with explicit inline code OR make FeatureCard a proper @Component (not @Builder) that takes @Prop fields, and invoke it normally.

Let me convert FeatureCard to a separate @Component struct `FeatureCardComp` with @Prop icon/tint/title/desc and onTap callback, and id. Then call `FeatureCardComp({ icon:'spin', tint:..., title:..., desc:..., id:..., onTap: ()=>... })`. Custom component calls DO accept these named params and it's the idiomatic pattern. The onTap arrow referencing this is fine.

But wait — custom component params: passing primitive @Prop values inline is fine. onTap as a callback field is fine. id is a universal attr, not a param. So I'd apply `.id(...)` after the component. Per memory, custom components (no @BuilderParam) can chain `.id()`. Good.

Let me refactor HomePage: create a FeatureCard component in UiKit (or inline in HomePage as a separate struct in same file). ArkTS allows multiple @Component structs per file? Actually a page file usually has one @Entry @Component; additional @Component structs in the same file ARE allowed. But to be safe, I'll add FeatureCard to UiKit.ets as an exported struct, and import it.

Let me add FeatureCard to UiKit and rewrite the quick-grid in HomePage to use it.

Also need to double-check: the build ONLY reported HomePage errors (13 errors, all in HomePage). That means all OTHER files compiled! Great — Store, services, all other pages, components compiled. Only HomePage's builder-object-literal pattern failed. Let me fix HomePage.

Let me add FeatureCard component to UiKit.ets.
